name: creating-claude-agents
description: Use when creating or improving Claude Code agents. Expert guidance on agent file structure, frontmatter, persona definition, tool access, model selection, and validation against schema.
capabilities: Guide on agent file structure and required paths
Explain frontmatter fields and validation rules
Recommend model selection based on task complexity
Show persona definition and content format requirements
Help troubleshoot agent activation and validation issues
author: AgentWorkforce
securitySummary: This skill is pure documentation containing only markdown guidance for creating Claude Code agents. No executable code, no network access, no file system operations. Static findings are false positives caused by the scanner misinterpreting markdown code blocks, documentation references, and YAML frontmatter as security patterns. All 179 detections are dismissed as benign documentation content.
valueStatement: Creating Claude Code agents requires understanding proper file structure, frontmatter syntax, and validation rules. This skill provides expert guidance to build agents that activate correctly and follow best practices for tool access, persona definition, and schema compliance.
limitations: Does not create agent files automatically
Does not execute or test created agents
Does not modify existing code or configurations
Does not integrate with external services or APIs

useCases.title: Build Custom AI Assistants
useCases.description: Create specialized Claude Code agents for specific tasks like code review, debugging, or documentation generation.

useCases.title: Standardize Agent Workflows
useCases.description: Establish consistent agent patterns across your team with proper structure, tool access, and validation.

useCases.title: Create Publishable Agents
useCases.description: Build agents that conform to schema requirements and can be distributed through the skill marketplace.

promptTemplates.title: Create New Agent
promptTemplates.scenario: Need a new Claude Code agent
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Claude Code agent for code review that checks for security vulnerabilities and performance issues.

promptTemplates.title: Fix Validation Errors
promptTemplates.prompt: My agent file is failing validation. Check the frontmatter and content format against the schema requirements.

promptTemplates.title: Configure Tools
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to configure an agent with read-only access and git commands only using the allowed-tools field.

promptTemplates.title: Model Selection
promptTemplates.prompt: Should I use sonnet, opus, or haiku for an agent that analyzes architecture decisions? Explain the trade-offs.

bestPractices: Define a clear, specific persona immediately after the H1 heading using 'You are...' format
Grant minimal tool access following the principle of least privilege
Include concrete examples showing both good and bad patterns for your agent's domain
antiPatterns: Using underscores or uppercase letters in agent names violates schema validation
Granting all tools to every agent creates unnecessary security risks
Vague agent descriptions like 'A helpful agent' prevent proper activation

faq.question: What file path do agents require?
faq.answer: Agents must be placed in .claude/agents/ directory as markdown files with .md extension.

faq.question: What fields are required in frontmatter?
faq.answer: The name and description fields are required. Name must use lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only.

faq.question: How do I restrict which tools an agent can use?
faq.answer: Use the allowed-tools field with comma-separated tool names. For Bash, use patterns like Bash(git *) to restrict commands.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using agents?
faq.answer: Agents only access files and tools you explicitly grant. This skill is read-only documentation that does not access your data.

faq.question: Why is my agent not activating?
faq.answer: Check that the file is in .claude/agents/, frontmatter is valid YAML, and description specifically matches your use case.

faq.question: How is this different from skills or slash commands?
faq.answer: Agents have persistent context and tool access for complex workflows. Skills provide context-aware documentation. Slash commands are for quick manual invocations.
